title: Elytranthe pallens
taxonomy: Elytranthe pallens is classified in the family Apocynaceae, a large and diverse group of flowering plants commonly known as the dogbane or oleander family. This classification is based on the plant's morphology and molecular characteristics, which are similar to those of other members of the Apocynaceae. The genus Elytranthe is a monotypic genus, meaning it contains only one species, Elytranthe pallens. Within the Apocynaceae family, Elytranthe pallens is further grouped into the subfamily Rauvoloideae. The classification of Elytranthe pallens is subject to ongoing research and revision.

Traditionally, it is used to treat various health conditions, including fever, rheumatism, and skin infections. The plant's leaves and stems are also used in folk medicine to treat respiratory issues, such as colds and bronchitis. intro: Elytranthe pallens, also known as White Snake Root, is a perennial plant species belonging to the Apocynaceae family. Traditionally, White Snake Root has been used to treat various health issues, including fever, rheumatism, and respiratory problems, due to its anti-inflammatory and antispasmodic properties. The plant contains bioactive constituents such as cardenolides, which are responsible for its medicinal properties, as well as other compounds like glycosides and phenolic acids. Elytranthe pallens is commonly used to make herbal preparations like teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are often used to relieve respiratory issues, such as bronchitis and asthma, as well as to reduce fever and alleviate rheumatic pain.
